name: Current Affairs in Indian Polity (11): JPC on 'One Nation, One Election' Gets Extension to Monsoon 2026

sourceRefs.label: newsonair.gov.in/manoramayearbook.in - Lok Sabha's mid-March 2026 extension of the JPC examining 'One Nation, One Election' bills, to Monsoon Session 2026

researchNotes: Fourteenth Polity current-affairs topic (11th lap). 'One Nation, One Election' is core, frequently-tested Polity content — this topic anchors on the dated 2026 JPC extension while covering the underlying bills and committee composition.

reading.intro: Covers the 'One Nation, One Election' bills' Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) and its 2026 tenure extension.

qa.question: Which 2 bills is the JPC on 'One Nation, One Election' examining?
qa.answer: The Constitution (129th Amendment) Bill, and the Union Territories Laws (Amendment) Bill — both aiming to synchronise national and state elections.

qa.question: When was the JPC constituted, and who chairs it?
qa.answer: Constituted in December 2024 by Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla; chaired by P.P. Chaudhary (BJP), a former Union Minister of State for Law and Justice.

qa.question: What is the JPC's total membership, and its composition across the 2 Houses?
qa.answer: 39 members total — 27 from the Lok Sabha and 12 from the Rajya Sabha.

qa.question: What extension did the Lok Sabha grant the JPC in March 2026, and by when must it now report?
qa.answer: The JPC's tenure was extended (approved mid-March 2026) — it must now submit its report by the first day of the last week of the Monsoon Session 2026.

qa.question: What was the Lok Sabha's vote count when the 'One Nation, One Election' Bill was first introduced (17 December 2024)?
qa.answer: 269 members in favour, 198 opposed (a division vote).

questions.prompt: The JPC on 'One Nation, One Election' examines the Constitution (129th Amendment) Bill and which other Bill?
